user_outcome: Real-binary temporary-Git tests prove complete PASS, failure, tamper, restart, merge, re-plan and ship behavior through public entry points.
rationale: Package tests cannot prove that every adapter reaches the same v0.15 authorities or that recovery survives process boundaries. This final slice adds no product behavior; it drives the built binary and MCP transport through complete disposable Git histories and fails closed on any divergence from C-01 through C-12.
in_scope: Build the current sworn binary once per conformance test suite and invoke it as a subprocess with isolated HOME, Git config, repositories, worktrees, bare remotes, model-driver fakes, clocks, and environment; no test calls an internal operation as a substitute for its public entry point.

Prove the exact embedded Baton v0.15 pin and isolated Codex/Claude mirror parity, typed-reference ambiguity behavior, live protocol marker gating, canonical semantic identity, report reuse, lifecycle budgets, and S13 cutover through public commands.

Drive sworn loop and maintainability review/adjudicate through clean PASS, cycle-0 failure and resume, cycle-1 re-slice, valid FAIL durability, process termination, commit-without-push recovery, remote restart, and exact model-dispatch counts.

Drive CLI, autonomous-loop, and MCP track merge over disjoint, declared shared, stale-overlap, ordinary rollback, post-sync rollback, undeclared-conflict, and idempotent histories and compare resulting refs, parents, trees, statuses, reports, and exit/tool outcomes.

Drive sworn replan apply and MCP apply_plan_delta through a ratified delta, partial-propagation restart, and all-or-nothing pristine migration of an isolated committed copy of the seven local-first-account-safety records, proving the live downstream release remains unchanged.

Drive merge-release, read-only ship, and post-deployment mark-shipped through normal, tampered, non-deployed, already-complete, and idempotent histories, proving readiness and deployment remain distinct facts.

Record deterministic per-scenario evidence in test failures: invoked public command, redacted stdout/stderr, exit code, dispatch counts, before/after refs, commit parents, tree/blob identities, and release-record hashes.
out_of_scope: This slice changes no production code, protocol contract, schema, installer, command behavior, or release record outside its own planning and proof artefacts.
Tests make no network request to a model provider, Git host, Baton host, notification destination, or deployment service.
A failing conformance scenario is fixed in its owning S01–S17 slice; S18 does not weaken assertions or add compatibility bypasses.
Performance benchmarking, UI board delivery, webhook product delivery, and hosted commercial behavior are outside this conformance proof.

effort_complexity.rationale: The implementation is test-only but spans every public lifecycle, multi-process recovery, multiple Git histories, and exact evidence comparison without external services.

risks.description: A broad end-to-end suite becomes flaky because it depends on global HOME, Git configuration, real remotes, wall clock, or model/network availability.
risks.mitigation: Isolate every process environment and Git config, use local bare remotes and deterministic fake drivers/clocks, prohibit network, and assert no process-global state leaks between scenario tables.

risks.description: The conformance test duplicates internal expectations and passes even though a public adapter bypasses them.
risks.mitigation: Invoke only built-binary and MCP stdio entry points, derive expected identities independently from Git plumbing and committed bytes, and compare all adapters on the same fixtures.

risks.description: A failing scenario is made green by relaxing the final test rather than repairing its owning behavior.
risks.mitigation: Every scenario names its owning C-01 through C-12 contract and S01 through S17 slice; any product mismatch routes back to that owner and the final assertion remains unchanged.

acceptance_criteria.text: WHEN the real-binary complete-PASS scenario installs isolated Codex and Claude Baton mirrors from the vendored source, validates the live protocol marker and typed references, runs one clean role lifecycle, merges its track and release, passes read-only ship, and records deployment with mark-shipped THE SYSTEM SHALL exit 0 only at each authorized PASS point and produce exact C-01 through C-11 records, refs, parents, trees, reports, and terminal states (N-01 through N-10).

IF the tamper matrix deletes or rewrites a report, changes a blob OID, protocol marker, semantic path, fingerprint, lifecycle member, sync parent, shared result, rollback baseline, readiness fact, deploy containment, or ratified delta source identity THEN the next affected public command SHALL exit non-zero, name the owning C-01 through C-12 gate, perform zero unauthorized model calls, and preserve pre-command refs and bytes (N-01 through N-11).

WHEN the test kills and restarts separate sworn processes after report write, transition commit, failed push, partial track propagation, and completed transition THE SYSTEM SHALL reuse only exact committed evidence, complete only the missing authorized side effect, produce no duplicate report or commit, and match the contract's exact Implementer, Verifier, and Coach-related dispatch counts (C-05, C-06, C-10, C-12, N-04, N-05, N-10).

WHEN real-binary and MCP scenarios process disjoint sibling evidence, a C-07 declared shared file, stale overlap, ordinary rollback, post-sync rollback, and an undeclared conflict THE SYSTEM SHALL preserve disjoint bytes, install only the canonical shared blob, invalidate only the deterministically affected interval, require exact applicable rollback equality, and block every undeclared or ambiguous integration (C-07, C-08, C-09, N-06, N-07, N-08, N-09).

WHEN sworn replan apply and MCP apply_plan_delta receive the same ratified C-12 fixture THE SYSTEM SHALL produce identical owner-seeded commits, receipts, propagation, and restart behavior; WHEN migrating an isolated committed copy of the seven pristine local-first-account-safety slices THE SYSTEM SHALL update all seven or none, add no synthetic execution evidence, pass v0.15 schema, trace, and ambiguity gates, and leave the live downstream release byte-identical (C-02, C-03, C-12, N-02, N-10, N-11).

WHEN the same valid release is checked before deployment, after a deploy commit containing its release integration, and after the shipped transition THE SYSTEM SHALL keep ship read-only, let only mark-shipped record deployment, and revalidate both operations on idempotent rerun; IF deployment containment or current C-09 authority is absent THEN no shipped state SHALL be written (C-09, C-11, N-09, N-10).
